title: Tordylium carmeli
taxonomy: Tordylium carmeli is classified in the family Apiaceae, which includes a wide range of plants commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. The genus Tordylium is a group of flowering plants native to the Middle East and North Africa. intro: Tordylium carmeli, also known as Carmer's Hog's Fennel, is a perennial plant belonging to the Apiaceae family, native to the Middle East. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, making it beneficial for the treatment of various ailments such as indigestion and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tordylium carmeli include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, which are commonly used to harness its health benefits.
